title: Nigerian Lawmakers Move to Repeal and Replace Outdated Investment and Securities Act - FinancialEDGE
description: The Nigerian Senate has taken a significant step towards modernizing the country's investment and securities landscape. On Thursday, a public hearing was held at the National Assembly in Abuja to discuss a bill aimed at repealing the Investment and Securities Act 2007 and enacting a new Investment and Securities Bill 2024. Key Figures in Attendance
html:
The Nigerian Senate has taken a significant step towards modernizing the country’s investment and securities landscape.

On Thursday, a public hearing was held at the National Assembly in Abuja to discuss a bill aimed at repealing the Investment and Securities Act 2007 and enacting a new Investment and Securities Bill 2024.

Key Figures in Attendance

– Dr. Emomotimi Agama, Director General of the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)
– Senator Binus Yaroe, representative of the President of the Senate
– Senator Osita Izunaso, Chairman of the Senate Committee on Capital Market
– Senator Peter Jiya, Deputy Chairman of the Committee

The Proposed Bill

The new bill seeks to update the existing regulatory framework, providing the SEC with more powers to oversee the capital market.

The bill is expected to address various issues, including the registration of securities exchanges and capital trade points, and the conditions for registration.

Potential Impact

If passed, the Investment and Securities Bill 2024 will replace the outdated 2007 act, providing a more effective and efficient regulatory environment for investments and securities in Nigeria.

 

 

L-R: Director General, Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), Dr. Emomotimi Agama, representative of the President of the Senate, Senator Binus Yaroe, Chairman, Senate Committee on Capital Market, Senator Osita Izunaso and Deputy Chairman of the Committee, Senator Peter Jiya, during a public hearing on a bill for an act to repeal the investment and Securities act 2007 (acts 29,2007), and enact the investment and Securities bill 2024, held at the National Assembly in Abuja, on Thursday.
